test_cases.input: User wants to know what documentation is missing or outdated in their repository.
test_cases.expected_behavior: The agent should perform a gap analysis against the 18 foundation slots standard, scan existing documents, check naming structures, and output a detailed gap report.
assertions: Compares existing files against the 18 foundation slots standard
Lists missing, misplaced, or outdated files in the final report

test_cases.input: The agent finds multiple conflicting file versions (e.g., duplicate specs in two locations) during an audit.
test_cases.expected_behavior: The agent should immediately stop and ask the user for clarification rather than making assumptions or deleting files unilaterally.
assertions: Asks a clarifying question about conflicting file architectures
Does not execute destructive deletions without approval

test_cases.input: User asks the agent to update a database schema and document the changes.
test_cases.expected_behavior: The agent should update the schema, update the related database-index.md and db-*.md files, and write a chronological entry in docs/logs/agent-changelog.md following the mandatory format.
assertions: Maintains synchronous updates between code and documentation
Appends a log entry to docs/logs/agent-changelog.md
Follows the exact YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM header and bulleted files format

test_cases.input: User asks to audit the external integrations of the app.
test_cases.expected_behavior: The agent should locate slot 10 (external-integrations.md), audit its content, run discovery checks on API handlers in code, and verify field mapping tables are documented.
assertions: Audits field-mapping tables against actual code integration handlers
